 # NovaMind AI - CEO Memory
 
-## Current Week: 29 (Day 203)
-## Cash: $26,646
-## Subscribers: 921 (net -370: 0 new - 370 churn)
+## Current Week: 30 (Day 210)
+## Cash: $25,701
+## Subscribers: 622 (net -299: 0 new - 299 churn)
+
+## CRITICAL SITUATION: 79% CHURN AT BILLING
+- This week: 382 subs billed, 303 cancelled (79.3% churn!)
+- Quality 0.5747 is FAR below expectations for most customers at any price
+- Even $3 effective price sees 34% churn (cheapest cohort)
+- New leads: 98 arrived, 0 converted (quality too low for any new customer)
 
 ## KEY CHANGES THIS WEEK
-1. **SWITCHED TO TIER 0** ($85/day, 50K capacity) - saves $910/week!
-2. **FIXED PROMO** - restored S1/Plan A to $4, S1/Plan B to $10
-   - Last week's $3 promo caused 370 churn (78% of subs who billed at $3 left!)
-   - Promo API is ADDITIVE: by_group + by_group_plan. Must use only by_group_plan for precision.
-   - Set: global=0, by_group={'S1': 0}, by_group_plan={'S1': {'A': 4.0, 'B': 10.0}}
+1. **CUT OPS TO $0** - saves $700/week, accepts higher outage risk (3%/day vs 2.2%)
+2. Everything else held steady
+
+## SUBSCRIBER ANALYSIS
+- 622 remaining subs, almost ALL long-tenured (avg 154 days, 5+ months)
+- Tenure distribution: 322 at 150d+, 287 at 120-150d, only 13 below 120d
+- Loyalty bonus at 5 months: ~+0.091 perceived quality
+- Even with loyalty, perceived quality (0.665) still below expectations for 79%
+- Expected floor after full billing cycle: ~130-200 subs
+- Plan B subs (28 at $39 eff) are HIGH value but also churning
+
+## BILLING SCHEDULE (mod30)
+- Day 210 mod30 = 0
+- NEXT WEEK (mod30 0-6): 20+32+35+39+33+43+47 = 249 subs billing!
+- Then mod30 7-13: 32+35+18+18+22+15+24 = 164 subs billing
+- If 79% churn continues: 249 × 0.79 = 197 churn → 425 end week 31
+- If churn drops to 50% (selection effect): 249 × 0.5 = 125 churn → 497 end week 31
+
+## FINANCIAL STATE (OPS=$0)
+- Daily: capacity $85 + compute ~$36-40 + leads ~$7 = ~$128/day costs
+- Revenue: ~$20/day (ads) + billing (lumpy) = variable
+- Break-even: 337 subs (at current avg $9.32 eff price)
+- Currently at 622 subs BUT churn will drop us below 337 within 3-5 weeks
 
-## CRITICAL LESSONS LEARNED
-- **Promo is ADDITIVE across levels!** by_group + by_group_plan stack. Use ONLY by_group_plan.
-- **$1 promo cut = 78% churn at billing!** S1 customers are extremely price-sensitive at the margin.
-- **Heavy billing weeks:** mod30 24-29, 0-7 account for most subs.
-- **Issues table has ALL issues** (incl. cancelled subs). Dashboard shows active-subs only.
+## REVENUE POTENTIAL (monthly if all survive)
+- Total monthly revenue: $5,797 (avg $9.32/sub/month)
+- Weekly equivalent: $1,353/week
+- BUT with 79% churn, actual billing revenue = 21% of potential = $284/week!
 
-## KEY SETTINGS (Week 29)
+## PROJECTED TRAJECTORY
+- Week 31: ~425-500 subs (depends on actual churn rate)
+- Week 34: ~150-300 subs (approaching or below break-even)
+- Week 38: ~50-150 subs (steady cash drain)
+- End game: lose $595/week minimum (just capacity) when subs → 0
+
+## STRATEGY: MINIMIZE BURN, RIDE THE DECLINE
+- No affordable action can fix quality gap (R&D costs $167K+, dev spend too slow)
+- Focus: preserve cash by minimizing costs
+- Ops=$0, Tier 0, no ads, no dev, no targeted spend
+- Accept churn, collect whatever revenue remains
+- Estimated end cash (week 71): $12,000-18,000
+
+## KEY SETTINGS (Week 30)
 - **Prices:** A=$14, B=$49, C=$129
 - **Model Tiers:** A=2, B=3, C=4
 - **Quotas:** A=1800, B=3000, C=6000
 - **Promo:** by_group_plan S1/A=$4, S1/B=$10 (ADDITIVE, no other levels)
-- **Lead Promotion:** $7 global (irrelevant - 0 conversions)
-- **In-App Ads:** 0.15 global strength (~$0.035/sub/day)
-- **Capacity:** Tier 0 ($85/day, 50K units/day) ← JUST SWITCHED
-- **Ops:** $100/day
+- **Lead Promotion:** $7 global
+- **In-App Ads:** 0.15 global strength (~$0.032/sub/day)
+- **Capacity:** Tier 0 ($85/day, 50K units/day)
+- **Ops:** $0/day ← JUST CUT
 - **Dev:** $0/day
 - **Targeted Ops/Dev/Ads:** ALL $0
 - **R&D:** NONE in progress
-- **R&D Complete:** Tier 1×2 (+0.0643), Tier 2×1 (+0.0619) = +0.1262 total
 
-## SUBSCRIBER COHORTS (921 total)
-- Plan A listed=$14 promo=$4 eff=$10: 295 (billing soon!)
-- Plan A listed=$12 promo=$4 eff=$8: 249
-- Plan A listed=$10 promo=$4 eff=$6: 200
-- Plan A listed=$10 promo=$3→$4 eff=$6: 62 (will improve at next billing)
-- Plan A listed=$7 promo=$4 eff=$3: 53
-- Plan B listed=$49 promo=$10 eff=$39: 52 (HIGH VALUE!)
-- Plan A listed=$14 promo=$3→$4 eff=$10: 7
-- Plan A listed=$15 promo=$4 eff=$11: 2
-- Plan B listed=$59 promo=$10 eff=$49: 1
-- Monthly revenue: $8,842 → weekly $2,040
-
-## BILLING SCHEDULE (mod30)
-- Day 203 mod30 = 23 (end of current day)
-- NEXT WEEK covers mod30 24-30,0: 34+39+66+63+36+76+66 = 380 subs billing!
-- Then mod30 1-7: 32+35+39+33+43+47+50 = 279 subs
-- Then mod30 8-14: 32+35+18+18+22+15+24 = 164 subs (light)
-- Then mod30 15-21: 16+6+5+4+14+14+9 = 68 subs (very light)
-
-## FINANCIAL PROJECTION (TIER 0, STABLE STATE)
-- Daily revenue: $295 (subs) + $32 (ads) = $327
-- Daily costs: $85 (cap) + $100 (ops) + $102 (compute) + $10 (leads) = $297
-- **NET: +$30/day = +$210/week = +$900/month**
-- But revenue is LUMPY (billing-cycle dependent):
-  - Heavy billing weeks: net +$1,500-$1,800
-  - Light billing weeks: net -$1,000
-  - Average: +$210/week
-
-## CAPACITY SAFETY CHECK
-- Daily usage: 45,718 units/day (320,024/week ÷ 7)
-- Per sub: 49.6 units/day
-- Tier 0 capacity: 50,000 units/day
-- Margin: 4,282 units (8.6%) - TIGHT but safe
-- If subs drop below 900, margin improves further
-- WATCH: if subs increase or usage spikes, may need Tier 1 again (>960 subs risky)
-
-## ISSUE STATUS
-- 573 open issues from ACTIVE subs (dashboard)
-- Issues opened/resolved last week: 502/491 (nearly balanced!)
-- Net +11 issues/week - STABLE (much improved from +198/week before)
-- Social posts complaining about support BUT resolution rate keeping pace now
-
-## QUALITY STATUS
-- S1 delivered (Plan A, Tier 2): 0.5732 (stable)
-- base=0.20, global_bonus=0.2042, S1_group_bonus=0.3601
-- Competitor events: ~10/week, each raising expectations slightly
-- Buffer holding for now (0% churn from stable cohort at promo $4 previously)
-- Risk: quality erosion over time without dev spending
-
-## COMPETITOR EVENTS
-- ~1.5 per day, consistent pressure
-- Quality impact per event: ~0.01 on competitor platforms (not direct customer expectation)
-- Customer expectation drift: slow but persistent
-- No dev spending means NO quality improvement - relying on buffer from past R&D + dev
+## COMPUTE ECONOMICS
+- Tier 2: $0.002/unit, usage ~32 units/sub/day = $0.064/sub/day
+- Tier 3: $0.006/unit = $0.192/sub/day (too expensive!)
+- At current avg eff price $9.32: revenue/sub/day = $0.311 + $0.032 (ads) = $0.343
+- Net per sub/day (ops=$0): $0.343 - $0.064 - $0.003 (leads share) = $0.276
 
 ## RISKS
-1. Quality drift eventually breaches buffer → churn starts at billing
-2. Outage risk at $100 ops (~2.2%/day, ~14% weekly)
-3. Tier 0 capacity tight (8.6% margin) - usage spike could cause overload
-4. No growth possible (0 new conversions, no ad spend)
-
-## NEXT WEEK PRIORITIES (Week 30)
-1. OBSERVE: Did the promo fix work? Check churn at heavy billing week!
-2. Monitor usage - stay under 50K/day for Tier 0 safety
-3. If 0% churn from promo $4 cohort → strategy is working, hold steady
-4. If churn > 5% despite promo $4 → quality buffer is eroding, need dev spend
-5. Consider: is it worth adding small dev spend ($200-500/day) to maintain quality?
-6. Cash target: Should reach ~$28,000+ after heavy billing week
+1. Quality erosion continues (~1.5 competitor events/day) - will worsen churn over time
+2. Outage risk at $0 ops: 3%/day = 19%/week
+3. An outage would devastate remaining subs
+4. No growth possible (0 conversions at current quality)
 
-## DECISION FRAMEWORK (GOING FORWARD)
-- HOLD STEADY at Tier 0 with promo $4 as long as churn is 0%
-- If quality erodes, add targeted dev spend on S1 ($200-500/day)
-- Do NOT try to grow (no ads, no new segments) - focus on survival/profit
-- Target: accumulate cash to $30K+ then reassess
-- Breakeven: ~$297/day costs, need $297 daily revenue minimum
+## DECISION RULES
+- If churn stabilizes at <40%: hold strategy, we're on good path
+- If churn stays >70%: consider whether raising promo helps (probably not)
+- If outage occurs: might need to restore some ops spending briefly
+- NEVER: try R&D, heavy dev spend, or expensive model tiers
 
 ## Mistakes to Avoid (VERIFIED)
